description: Renovate is the project's ONLY update bot (full-Renovate switch, 2026-07). It AUTO-DETECTS
every ecosystem from the manifests (npm, pip, docker, actions, gradle, cargo, go, conan…) with
NO list to maintain — that is what makes the scope universal: a language added tomorrow is
covered without touching this file. Dependabot was dropped because it can NEITHER auto-detect
(every ecosystem must be declared by hand) NOR read a binary curl-ed inside a `run:`. Dependabot
ALERTS (CVE detection, native, free on private repos) STAY on: Renovate READS them to open the
security pull requests.
UPDATE POLICY — stay current, but through reviewed steps:
· ROUTINE version = a pull request reviewed by a human (top-level `automerge` left false).
· SECURITY = auto-merge (`vulnerabilityAlerts.automerge`). Security pull requests natively
BYPASS `minimumReleaseAge` (Renovate docs: "security updates bypass any minimumReleaseAge
checks"). The 3-day poisoned-release delay therefore applies to routine, NEVER to a CVE fix.
⚠ THIS KEY IS CALLED `description` — the ONLY one meant for comments. An UNKNOWN key makes
Renovate REJECT the whole config and STOP EVERY pull request, silently (the `_comment_role` bug,
test003, 2026-07-14). Validate every edit: `renovate-config-validator` (already run in CI).
⚠ The binaries' `*_VERSION` values carry the `v` prefix (v8.30.1, NOT 8.30.1): MANDATORY. The
`github-release-attachments` datasource resolves the digest via GET /releases/tags/{currentValue};
without the `v` → 404, Renovate gives up, no pull request — breaking nothing visibly
(renovatebot/renovate#27317). The digest is never guessed: Renovate finds the asset whose SHA256
matches the one already in place (via the release's checksums.txt). SAFETY NET: a wrong digest
makes `sha256sum -c` FAIL in CI.

packageRules.description: Minor/patch grouped so the reviewer is not drowned. MAJORS stay isolated: a major gets looked at on its own.
The 4 pinned scanner binaries (VERSION+SHA256 in a run:): their own group — a scanner major changes its rules, hence its findings.

description: renovate-config-validator: automerged, and out of the group above. It publishes ~6 times a
DAY — pinned exact, it would raise a mechanical pull request every week for nothing. It is
a CI LINT tool, never shipped at runtime, and its automerge is GUARDED by the very CI that
runs it: if an upstream version regresses, the config validation step goes red and the pull
request cannot merge. That is exactly the failure mode (a validator that LIES) the `>= 43`
floor was chosen for — caught BEFORE main instead of on it.
renovate-config-validator, pinned EXACT. It comes from npm, not from a GitHub release:
no `currentDigest` here — an npm version is IMMUTABLE, a checksum would add nothing.
The pin does matter: `renovate@43` re-resolved the newest 43.x ON EVERY RUN, so `main`
could go red without a line changing. A floating range does not only hand you the fixes,
it hands you the regressions too — with no known-good version to fall back to.
⚠ `check.sh` carries the same version, which is why it is in managerFilePatterns.
